notes: W9D1 — first day of the QL-recovery deload. Felt really good, really needed it, better than staying at home. Deload is working as intended — showing up, moving, not re-angering anything. Key discoveries: (1) Seated DB press felt great — rotating the DBs from facing toward face at bottom → facing away at top really helps the shoulder, new cue to keep. (2) 'Shoulder in front' form on incline DB (the new forearm-stacked-over-elbow cue) made 40lb way harder than expected — form change IS the progression. (3) Chest press at 150 deload was actually hard with slow tempo + full ROM — noticed the nagging right shoulder blade is probably a muscle connecting the shoulder to the right front pec (feels it all at once when chest pressing slow). Second set harder than first, got 6 on third and dropped to 130x4 to finish — classic deload 'lighter weight feels real when form is honest.' Kinesis scale: lat raise 2, incline overhead tri 3, straight arm 4.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Felt great. KEY CUE: rotate DBs from facing-in at bottom to facing-away at top — huge shoulder relief. New staple form.
With the new 'shoulder in front, stack forearm over elbow, elbows 45°' cue — way harder than expected. Finished all sets. The form is the progression, not the weight.
Set 1: hard with slow tempo + full ROM.
Dropped to 130 and finished 4 more. Right shoulder blade → front pec connection very noticeable at slow tempo — likely pec minor / subscapularis referring. Worth watching.
On Kinesis (not cable column). Scale is different from the regular cable column — 2 on Kinesis ≠ 35 on cables.
